concept.name: Asymmetric cryptography
concept.definition: A style of secret-keeping that uses two different keys instead of one shared password: one key locks a message and a separate, matching key unlocks it.

audience: a curious adult with no domain background
atomic_reason: The two-key idea clicks in one short figure: a padlock anyone can snap shut versus the single key that opens it, contrasted with one shared password. Everything it leans on, like locks, keys, and keeping a message secret, is everyday knowledge.
